dtcs.description: Driver's MICU Internal (EEPROM) Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B10A2 indicated? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Left Daytime Running Lights Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode. Is DTC B10CF indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the headlight high beam ON. Does the left headlight (high beam) come on? Go to step 7. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Measure the voltage between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (20P)   terminals No.1   and No. 2,   and between body ground and connector R (24P) terminals No. 2   and No. 5   individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G401,G402).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. 28 (10 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 12. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair the short to ground on the No. 28 (10 A) fuse circuit.
Check the left headlight bulb. Is the bulb OK? Go to step 13. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P).
Disconnect the left headlight (high beam) 2P connector.

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) terminal No. 13   and left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G302).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11A2 indicated? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passengers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passengers MICU) Removal and Installation. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Right Daytime Running Lights Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode. Is DTC B11CF indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the headlight high beam ON. Does the right headlight (high beam) come on? Go to step 7. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Measure the voltage between body ground and passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (38P)   terminals No. 36   and No. 37, and between body ground and passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P) terminals No. 15   individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passengers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passengers MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G203, G401, G405).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. 1 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 12. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair the short to ground on the No. 1 (10 A) fuse circuit.
Check the right headlight bulb. Is the bulb OK? Go to step 13. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P).
Disconnect the right headlight (high beam) 2P connector.

Check for continuity between passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P) terminal No. 2   and right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passengers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passengers MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G203).

dtcs.description: Passenger's MICU IG1 Line Input Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11C7 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections. If the connections are good, check the battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Battery Test   and the charging system.
Check the DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11C7 indicated with DTC U1282? Go to DTC U1282 troubleshooting  See: U Code Charts/U1282. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (38P) terminal No. 35 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passengers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passengers MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box and the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box.

dtcs.description: Power Control Unit Internal (CPU) Error

steps.instruction: Do the battery terminal disconnect procedure.
Wait for at least 10 seconds to clear the DTCs.
Do the battery terminal reconnection procedure.
Press and hold the engine start/stop button.
Wait for at least 10 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12A1 indicated? Replace the power control unit. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Engine Start/Stop Switch Failure

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Press the engine start/stop button at least 20 times, then wait for at least 20 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12C5 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Do the engine start/stop switch test  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Start / Stop Switch/Testing and Inspection. Is the engine start/stop switch OK? Go to step 5. Replace the engine start/stop switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Start / Stop Switch/Testing and Inspection .
Reconnect the engine start/stop switch 10P connector.

Measure the voltage between body ground and engine start/stop switch 10P connector terminal No. 1. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 7. Check the No. 12 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box. If the fuse is OK, check for an open in the wire.
Press and hold the engine start/stop button.

Measure the voltage between body ground and engine start/stop switch 10P connector terminal No. 4. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G405).
Disconnect the engine start/stop switch 10P connector.
Disconnect power control unit connector C (36P).

Check for continuity between engine start/stop switch 10P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 5,   and power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 8 and No. 7   respectively. Is there continuity? Replace the power control unit. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: Electric Steering Lock Switch C/D Monitor Circuit Failure

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Press the engine start/stop button at least 10 times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12C6 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 12. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 5. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G405).
Disconnect the electric steering lock 12P   connector.
Disconnect power control unit connector C (36P).
Check for continuity between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminals No. 9 and No. 10   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 8.

Check for continuity between power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 29 and No. 30, and electric steering lock 12P connector terminals No. 10   and No. 9 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect power control unit connector C (36P).
Select PCU from the ONE-PUSH START system select menu with the HDS, and enter DATA LIST.
Check each PCU value with the DATA LIST menu.

Connect the jumper wires to body ground and to electric steering control unit 12P connector terminals No. 10   and No. 9. Are all data list values correct? Replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Replace the power control unit.

dtcs.description: Electric Steering Lock Motor Circuit Failure

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12C7 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Disconnect the engine start/stop switch 10P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Go to step 6. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G405).
Disconnect the electric steering lock 12P   connector.
Disconnect power control unit connector C (36P).
Check for continuity between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminals No. 3 and No. 5   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 31 and No. 32, and electric steering lock 12P connector terminals No. 5 and No. 3 respectively. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good power control unit. If the symptom goes away, replace the original power control unit. If not, replace the electric steering lock See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: , B12D1, B12D2, B12D3 DTC B12D0: Power Control Unit IG1A Circuit Error DTC B12D1: Power Control Unit IG1B Circuit Error DTC B12D2: Power Control Unit IG2 Circuit Error DTC B12D3: Power Control Unit IG Latch Circuit Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B12D0, B12D1, B12D2, and/or B12D3 indicated? Replace the power control unit. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure at least 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12E0 indicated? Replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: , B12E2, B12E3, B12E4 DTC B12E1: Electric Steering Lock Motor Internal Error DTC B12E2: Electric Steering Lock Low Level Failure of MTRCUT1 DTC B12E3: Electric Steering Lock High Level Failure of MTRCUT1 DTC B12E4: Electric Steering Lock High Level Failure of MTRCUT2

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 11 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B12E1, B12E2, B12E3, and/or B12E4 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Disconnect the electric steering lock 12P   connector.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 4. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 6. Blown No. 12 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box, repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect the electric steering lock 12P connector.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 12. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G405).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 7. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or blown No. 9 (20 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.
Disconnect keyless access control unit connector A (36P).

Check for continuity between electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 6 and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Go to step 14. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 6 and keyless access control unit connector A (36P) terminal No. 27. Is there continuity? Go to step 15. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Disconnect power control unit connector C (36P).

Check for continuity between electric steering lock 12P connector terminals No. 5   and No. 3   and power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 31   and No. 32 respectively. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good electric steering lock. If the symptom goes away, replace the original electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. If not, replace the remote slot control unit. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: Power Control Unit Lost Communication With Electric Steering Lock

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12F0 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 4. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 5. Blown No. 12 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box, repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 12. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 6. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G405).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect these items: - Electric steering lock 12P   connector - Power control unit connector C (36P) - Remote slot control unit 40P connector - Keyless access control unit connector B (32P)
Check for continuity between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 1 and power control unit connector C (36P) terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect these items: - Electric steering lock 12P connector - Power control unit connector C (36P) - Remote slot control unit 40P connector - Keyless access control unit connector B (32P)
Do the battery terminal disconnect procedure.
Wait for at least 10 seconds to clear the DTCs.
Do the battery terminal reconnection procedure.
Turn the keyless access system mode switch ON.
If necessary, remove the remote from the remote slot.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode, then select the OFF mode.
Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Press and hold the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode. Does the mode change to ON? Go to step 19. Go to step 22.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12F0 indicated? Replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Connect the HDS to the data link connector (DLC).
Select COMMUNICATION STATUS in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS.

Check the value of STATUS with ESL, STATUS with KEYLESS ACCESS CONTROL UNIT, and STATUS with SLOT with the HDS. Are only values of status with ESL 1? Replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Replace the power control unit.

dtcs.description: Power Control Unit Lost Communication With Remote Slot Control Unit

steps.instruction: Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B12F1 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select ONE-PUSH START from the SYSTEM SELECTION MENU with the HDS.
Select the REMOTE SLOT CONTROL UNIT and check for DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs indicated? Go to the indicated DTCs troubleshooting. Go to step 7.

dtcs.description: Right BSI Alert Indicator Output Error

Measure the resistance between right BSI alert indicator 2P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. There should be an open circuit or at least 1 MOhms. Is the resistance as specified? dtcs.description: Driver's MICU IG1 Line Input Error

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1036 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections at the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   connector C (5P). If the connections are good, check the battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Battery Test   and the charging system.

Measure the voltage between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 15   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation. dtcs.description: Wiper Auto-Stop (As) Signal Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Turn the wiper switch to LOW or HIGH for at least 15 seconds, then turn the wiper switch OFF.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1077 indicated? Check for loose or poor connections at the driver's MICU   and the wiper motor. If the connections are OK, replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test. Is the windshield wiper motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair, and recheck.
Disconnect the windshield wiper motor 4P connector.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P).

Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3 and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) terminal No. 9. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 4P   connector terminal No. 3   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 11.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3   and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire. Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Measure the voltage between under-hood fuse/relay box connector C (5P) terminal No. 4   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 16. Go to step 15.

Check the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse circuit. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground in the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse circuit.
Do the relay circuit board test. Is the relay circuit board OK? Go to step 17. Replace the relay circuit board, and recheck.
Disconnect under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P).

Check for continuity between under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 4   and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 19. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G301).
Do the windshield wiper motor test Is the windshield wiper motor OK? Go to step 20. Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair, and recheck.
Reconnect under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between body ground and windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 2   with the wiper switch ON (Low), and measure the voltage between body ground and windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 4   with the wiper switch ON (High) individually. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the windshield wiper motor and body ground. If the wire is OK, check for poor ground (G301). Go to step 23.

Measure the voltage between body ground and under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 5   with the wiper switch ON (Low), and measure the voltage between body ground and under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 6   with the wiper switch ON (High) individually. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open or high resistance in the BLU (LO) or YEL (HI) wire. Check terminals F7   and F8 of the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) using the input test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wipers/Washers (Drivers and  Passengers MICU) Input Test. If the input tests prove OK, replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Drivers Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Drivers MICU) Removal and Installation.

Is there continuity? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's power window motor.

Check for continuity between driver's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 3, No. 5,   and No. 6   and body ground individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 12.

Test the driver's power window motor  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Power Window Motor Test. Is the motor OK? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the front passenger's power window switch 37P connector.
Disconnect the front passenger's power window motor 6P connector.

Check for continuity between front passenger's power window switch 37P connector terminals No. 26,No. 27, and No. 28 and passenger's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 5,No. 6,   and No. 3 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the front passenger's window switch and the front passenger's power window motor.

Check for continuity front passenger's window motor 6P connector between terminals No. 3, No. 5, and No. 6   and body ground individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 12.

Test the front passenger's power window motor  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Front Passenger's Power Window Motor Test. Is the motor OK? Replace the front passenger's power window switch.  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger's Power Window Switch Replacement Replace the front passenger's power window motor; replace the front passenger's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Driver's Power Window Position Detect Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Open and close the driver's power window by using the driver's switch manually.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1125 or B1126 indicated? Troubleshoot DTC B1125 or B1126  See: B1125. Go to step 5.
Reset the power window control unit  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Windows and Glass/Power Window Control Module/Testing and Inspection.
Check for DTCs again with the HDS. Is DTC B1140 indicated? Go to step 7. The system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connectors.
Substitute a known-good power window master switch.
Open and close the driver's power window by using the driver's switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1140 indicated? Faulty the driver's power window motor; replace the driver's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair. Faulty door multiplex control unit; replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement.

dtcs.description: Door Multiplex Control Unit Lost Communication With Other Unit (UART communication error)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Open and close all power windows by using the power window master switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1142 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Disconnect these items: - Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector. - Disconnect the front passenger's power window switch 37P connector. - Disconnect the moonroof control unit/motor 14P connector.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27 and front passenger's power windows switch 37P connector terminal No. 29. Is there continuity? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27 and moonroof control unit 14P connector terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 10.
Reconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27   and body ground. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to the front passenger's power window switch input test, and do the power and ground input tests  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Power Window Master Switch Input Test. If the tests prove OK, substitute a known good front passenger's power window switch. If the DTC goes away, replace the original front passenger's power window switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger's Power Window Switch Replacement. If the DTC is still present, replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Repair a short to power in the wire.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1130 or B1131 indicated? Troubleshoot DTC B1130 or B1131  See: B1130. Go to step 5.

Reset the power window control unit and the front passenger's power window control unit  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Windows and Glass/Power Window Control Module/Testing and Inspection.
Check for DTCs again with the HDS. Is DTC B1145 indicated? Go to step 7. The system has recovered at this time.
Substitute a known-good passenger's power window switch.
Open and close the front passenger's power window by using the front passenger's switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1145 indicated? Faulty the front passenger's power window motor; replace the front passenger's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair. Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1152 indicated? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait at least than 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTCs B1175 indicated? - Without SH-AWD: Go to step 5. - With SH-AWD: Go to step 10. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 4 and fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminals No. 3 and No. 1 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Do the fuel gauge sending unit test  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Tank Unit/Testing and Inspection. Is the fuel gauge sending unit OK? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Replace fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.
Disconnect the fuel gauge sending 4P connector.
Disconnect the secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.

Check for continuity between fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminal No. 3 and secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 2 and fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminal No. 3. Is there continuity? Go to step 15. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 4 and secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Do the fuel gauge sending unit test  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Tank Unit/Testing and Inspection. Is the fuel tank unit OK? Go to step 17. Replace fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.

Do the secondary fuel gauge sending unit test  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Testing and Inspection. Is the secondary fuel gauge sending unit OK? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Replace the secondary gauge sending unit  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Service and Repair/Secondary Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Removal/Installation.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Short

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the fuel level sensor circuit is OK at this time. Check for worn/missing insulation or an internal short in the wire.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 11. Replace the fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 4   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the gauge control module   and the fuel gauge sending unit. - Without SH-AWD: Go to step 14. - With SH-AWD: Go to step 15.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 2   and No. 4. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the gauge control module   and the fuel gauge sending unit. Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair.
Disconnect the secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.
Check for continuity between fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminals No. 1   and No. 3. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire. Go to step 17.
Reconnect the fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.
Reconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 23. Replace the secondary fuel gauge sending unit  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Fuel Gauge Sender/Service and Repair/Secondary Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector.

Check for continuity between secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the fuel gauge sending unit   and the secondary fuel gauge sending unit. Go to step 27.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between secondary fuel gauge sending unit 4P connector terminals No. 1   and No. 3. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire. Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With the Battery Sensor (UART LINE)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Wait for at least 15 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1192 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the battery sensor   is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the battery sensor and the gauge control module.
Check the No. 12 (7.5 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 5. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. 12 (7.5 A)   fuse circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the battery sensor 2P connector.

Measure the voltage between battery sensor 2P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the under-hood   fuse/relay box and the battery sensor.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Measure the voltage between battery sensor 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the gauge control module   and the battery sensor. Go to step 10.

Check for continuity between battery sensor 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the battery sensor   and the gauge control module. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between battery sensor 2P connector terminal No. 1   and gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Replace gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open in the wire between the gauge control module   and the battery sensor.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator J and MODE: An Open or Short in the Recirculation Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then ON (II)   or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode, and then the ON mode.
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1215 or J and MODE indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the recirculation control motor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Test the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Recirculation Control Motor Test. Is the recirculation control motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Recirculation Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the recirculation control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector C (20P) and the recirculation control motor 7P connector. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor.

Check for continuity between body ground and climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 18,   No. 19, and No. 20 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. Go to step 11.
Check for continuity between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals as follows. Is there continuity between any of the terminals? Repair a short in the wire(s). Go to step 12.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode, and measure the voltage between body ground and climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 18, No. 19 and No. 20 individually. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector C (20P) and at the recirculation control motor   7P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1802 indicated? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Slide Motor Pulse Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the power seat adjustment switch for several seconds in each direction.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1825 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1836 also indicated? Go to the DTC B1836 troubleshooting  See: B1836. Go to step 6.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1826, B1827, and/or B1828 also indicated? Go to step 17. Go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the slide switch for 2 seconds, and check the slide motor operation. Does the slide motor run smoothly? Go to step 9. Go to step 24.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 29 and No. 11 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 3 and No. 4 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 14.
Reconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 4 (+) and No. 3 (-). Is there about 5 V? Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. If there is no voltage, replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. If there is battery   voltage, repair a short in the wire (sensor line).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 15 and No. 29 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 3 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 21. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 15 and No. 29. Is there battery voltage when the slide switch is operated? Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement.

Check the No. 19 (20 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 25. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair the short to ground on the No. 19 (20 A) fuse circuit.

Measure the voltage between body ground and power seat control unit connector A (18P) terminal No. 8. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 26. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   and the power seat control unit.

Measure the voltage between body ground at power seat control unit connector A (18P) terminal No. 18 when operating the slide switch. Is there 0.2 V or more? Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the power seat control unit   and body ground (G701). Go to step 27.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector A (18P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector A (18P) terminals No. 17 and No. 7 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 5 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 31. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 5 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 32.

Test the slide motor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Power Seat Motor Test. Is the motor OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement.
