dtcs.description: Left Mirror Position Sensor Open

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the left power mirror in each direction with the power mirror switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1830 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Remove the driver's door panel  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Panel/Service and Repair/Front Door Panel Removal / Installation.
Disconnect the power mirror control unit 24P connector.
Disconnect the left power mirror 16P connector.
Check for continuity between body ground and left power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 6. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 10.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 7 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and left power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 6. Is there continuity? Go to step 12. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and left power mirror 16P connector terminals No. 8 and No. 13   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 13.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminals No. 4 and No. 5 to body ground with jumper wires.

Check for continuity between body ground and left power mirror 16P connector terminals No. 8 and No. 13   individually. Is there continuity? Go to step 15. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 6. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 16.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 6 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and left power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 7. Is there continuity? Go to step 18. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect the power mirror control unit 24P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between power mirror control unit 24P connector terminals No. 6 and No. 7. Is there about 5 V? Faulty left power mirror position sensor; replace the power mirror actuator  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Power Mirror Motor/Service and Repair. Substitute a known-good power mirror control unit, and recheck. If DTC is gone, the original power mirror control unit is faulty; replace it  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Testing and Inspection/Power Mirror Control Unit Input Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Right Mirror Position Sensor Open

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the right power mirror in each direction with the power mirror switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1832 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Remove the front passenger's door panel  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Panel/Service and Repair/Front Door Panel Removal / Installation.
Disconnect the power mirror control unit 24P connector.
Disconnect the right power mirror 16P connector.
Check for continuity between body ground and right power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 6. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 10.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 7 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and right power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 6. Is there continuity? Go to step 12. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and right power mirror 16P connector terminals No. 8 and No. 13   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 13.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminals No. 19 and No. 20 to body ground with jumper wires.

Check for continuity between body ground and right power mirror 16P connector terminals No. 8 and No. 13   individually. Is there continuity? Go to step 15. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 6 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 16.
Connect power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 6 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and right power mirror 16P connector terminal No. 7. Is there continuity? Go to step 18. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Reconnect the power mirror control unit 24P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between power mirror control unit 24P connector terminals No. 6 and No. 7. Is there about 5 V? Faulty right power mirror position sensor; replace the power mirror actuator  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Power Mirror Motor/Service and Repair. Substitute a known-good power mirror control unit, and recheck. If DTC is gone, the original power mirror control unit is faulty; replace it  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Testing and Inspection/Power Mirror Control Unit Input Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Position Sensor Circuit Short

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the power seat adjustment switch in all directions for more than 1 second each.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1836 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the 5P connector from each motor.
Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminal No. 15. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between slide motor/position sensor terminal No. 2 and the motor body. Is there continuity? Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. Go to step 10.

Check for continuity between front up-down motor/position sensor, rear up-down motor/position sensor, and recline motor/position sensor terminal No. 3 and motor body. Is there continuity? If the front or rear up-down motor/position sensor is faulty, replace the seat frame assembly  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat/Front Seat Frame Replacement. If the recline motor/position sensor is faulty, replace the recline motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Recline Motor Replacement. Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Memory Position Calling Time-out

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Move both power mirrors to the full up and full left positions with the power mirror switch.
Move the power seat to the full front up and full rear up positions, then slide the seat all the way back and completely recline the seat-back backward with the power seat adjustment switch.
Set the seating position into memory by pressing either the Driver 1 or the Driver 2 position memory switch.
Using the power seat switches, move the seat away from the position just memorized.
Recall the memory position with the driving position memory switch.
Wait for 2 minutes.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs indicated? Go to step 10. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs other than B1837 indicated? Go to the indicated DTCs troubleshooting. Go to step 11.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Wait for 6 seconds.

Move each power seat motor one at a time, and check the operation time. Does any motor fail to run smoothly? Go to the power seat motor test  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Power Seat Motor Test. If the motor is OK, check the engagement of the motor gear. Go to the power seat control unit input test  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: UART Communication Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the power seat with the driving position memory switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1843 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the power seat control unit connectors and power mirror control unit 24P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminal No. 38 and power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 13. Is there continuity? Go to step 8. Repair an open in the wire between the power mirror control unit   and power seat control unit.
Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminal No. 38. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.
Reconnect the power mirror control unit 24P connector.

Measure the voltage between body ground and power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 24. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 11. Check No. 15 (10 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay   box. If the fuse is OK, repair an open in or high resistance the wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between body ground and power mirror control unit 24P connector terminal No. 22. Is there less than 0.2 V? Replace the power mirror control unit  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Testing and Inspection/Power Mirror Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the power mirror control unit   and body ground (G401). If the wire is OK, repair G401.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Slide Switch Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the slide switch in each direction for more than 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1844 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the power seat adjustment switch 12P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 1,No. 7,   and No. 24 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between these power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals: - No. 1 and No. 7 - No. 1 and No. 24 - No. 7 and No. 24 Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire(s). Go to step 10.

Test the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement. Is the switch OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Replace the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Recline Switch Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the recline switch in each direction for more than 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1845 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the power seat adjustment switch 12P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 1,No. 6,   and No. 23 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between these power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals: - No. 1 and No. 6 - No. 1 and No. 23 - No. 6 and No. 23 Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire(s). Go to step 10.

Test the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement. Is the switch OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Replace the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Front Up-Down Switch Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the front up-down switch in each direction for more than 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1846 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the power seat adjustment switch 12P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 1,No. 4,   and No. 5 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between these power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals: - No. 1 and No. 4 - No. 1 and No. 5 - No. 4 and No. 5 Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire(s). Go to step 10.

Test the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement. Is the switch OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Replace the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Power Seat Rear Up-Down Switch Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Operate the rear up-down switch in each direction for more than 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1847 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (40P).
Disconnect the power seat adjustment switch 12P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals No. 1,No. 3,   and No. 22 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between these power seat control unit connector B (40P) terminals: - No. 1 and No. 3 - No. 1 and No. 22 - No. 3 and No. 22 Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire(s). Go to step 10.

Test the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement. Is the switch OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Power Seat Control Unit Input Test/Replacement. Replace the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement.

dtcs.description: Remote Slot Control Unit Internal Error (EEPROM Error)

steps.instruction: Do the battery terminal disconnect procedure.
Wait for at least 10 seconds to clear the DTCs.
Do the battery terminal reconnection procedure.
Push the door outer handle lock button to lock the door.
Touch the door outer handle touch sensor to unlock the door.
Connect the HDS to the data link connector (DLC).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode according to the instructions on the HDS screen.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1902 indicated? Replace the remote slot control unit. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Remote Slot Antenna Open or Short

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Do the procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Select the ONE-PUSH START from the SYSTEM SELECTION MENU with the HDS.

Select the SLOT UNIT and check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1926 indicated? Replace the remote slot  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

dtcs.description: MTR CONT Signal Error

steps.instruction: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the electric steering lock 12P connector.
Measure the voltage between body ground and electric steering lock 12P connector terminal No. 6. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 4. Go to step 8.
Disconnect keyless access control unit connector A (36P).
Measure the voltage between body ground and keyless access control unit connector A (36P) terminal No. 27. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 6. Replace the keyless access control unit.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.

Measure the voltage between body ground and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminal No. 12. Is there battery voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire. Replace the remote slot control unit.
Reconnect the electric steering lock 12P connector.
Disconnect keyless access control unit connector A (36P).

Measure the voltage between body ground and keyless access control unit connector A (36P) terminal No. 27. Is there battery voltage? Replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Go to step 11.
Reconnect keyless access control unit connector A (36P).
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1927 indicated? Substitute a known-good remote   slot control unit. If the symptom goes away, replace the original remote slot control unit. If not, substitute a known-good keyless access control unit. If the symptom goes away, replace the original keyless access control unit. If not, replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Go to step 15.
Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Do this procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1927 indicated? Substitute a known-good remote   slot control unit. If the symptom goes away, replace the original remote slot control unit. If not, substitute a known-good keyless access control unit. If the symptom goes away, replace the original keyless access control unit. If not, replace the electric steering lock  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Steering Column Lock/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

dtcs.description: Remote Slot Control Unit LOCK/UNLOCK Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the keyless access system mode switch OFF.
Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Do the procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Select the ONE-PUSH START from the SYSTEM SELECTION MENU with the HDS.
Select the SLOT UNIT and check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1930 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.
Disconnect the remote slot 16P connector.
Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 5 and No. 25 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 5 and No. 25 and remote slot 16P connector terminals No. 16 and No. 8 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 12. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminal No. 13. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 13.

Check for continuity between remote slot control unit 40P connector terminal No. 8 and remote slot 16P connector terminal No. 13. Is there continuity? Go to step 14. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminals No. 7 and No. 15 individually. Is there continuity? Check for loose or poor connections at the remote   slot control unit and the remote slot. If the connections are OK, substitute a known-good remote slot. If the symptom goes away, replace the original remote slot  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement. If not, replace the remote slot control unit. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G403).

dtcs.description: Remote Slot Control Unit no Response

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the keyless access system mode switch OFF.
Insert the remote into the remote slot.
Do the procedure 10 times: Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, ON, and OFF mode.
Select the ONE-PUSH START from the SYSTEM SELECTION MENU with the HDS.
Select the SLOT UNIT and check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1931 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Check for loose connections in the remote slot 16P connector. Is the connections OK? Go to step 9. Replace the faulty connections, and then recheck the system.

Measure the voltage between body ground and the remote slot 16P connector terminal No. 1. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 10. Check for blown No. 12 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box   and an open or a short to ground in the wire. If the fuse and wire are OK, replace the remote slot  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement.
Disconnect the remote slot 16P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminals No. 7 and No. 15 individually. Is there continuity? Go to step 12. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G403).
Reconnect the remote slot 16P connector.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.
Measure the voltage between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminal No. 11. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 18. Go to step 15.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Substitute a known-good remote   slot. If the symptom goes away, replace the original remote slot  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement. If not, replace the remote slot control unit.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.
Disconnect the remote slot 16P connector.
Check for continuity between body ground and remote slot 16P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 9   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 22.

Check for continuity between remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 9,No. 29,   and No. 28,   and remote slot 16P   connector terminals No. 2,No. 9,   and No. 11   respectively. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good remote   slot. If the symptom goes away, replace the original remote slot  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement. If not, replace the remote slot control unit. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: XM Antenna line failure

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Troubleshoot the AcuraLink.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode. and then back to ON (II), or the ON mode and wait 30 seconds   or more.

Check for DTC with the HDS. Is DTC B2240 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the XM antenna   is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver)   and the XM antenna.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Check for poor connections or loose terminals at AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver) connector B (2P) and the XM antenna 2P connector. Are the connections or terminals OK? Go to step 7. Repair poor connections or loose terminals, and recheck the DTC.
Substitute a known-good XM antenna  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode, and then back to ON (II), or the ON mode and wait 30 seconds or more.

Check for DTC with the HDS. Is DTC B2240 indicated? Update the AcuraLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning   if it does not have the latest software. If the software is the latest, replace the original AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Service and Repair/AcuraLink Control Unit (XM Receiver) Removal/Installation . Replace the original XM antenna  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Radio/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator A and Windshield Defrost: An Open in the Humidity Sensor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II),   or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2967 or A and WINDSHIELD DEFROST indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, check for loose wires or poor connections on the humidity sensor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Remove the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair, and test it  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Humidity Sensor/Testing and Inspection. Is the humidity sensor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair.
Disconnect the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector C (20P) and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector. Is there continuity? Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector C (20P) and at the humidity/in-car temperature sensor   4P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the humidity sensor.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator B and Windshield Defrost: A Short in the Humidity Sensor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II),   or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2968 or B and WINDSHIELD DEFROST indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the humidity sensor circuit.
Check for DTCs. Are these DTCs also present; B1234 or K and AUTO, and/or B1237 or N and AUTO, and/or B2980   or F and WINDSHIELD DEFROST? Go to step 13. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Remove the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair, and test it  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Humidity Sensor/Testing and Inspection. Is the humidity sensor OK? Go to step 8. Replace the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair.
Disconnect the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit   and the humidity sensor. Go to step 11.
Check for continuity between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 2 and No. 12. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires. Go to step 12.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode, and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector C (20P)   terminal No. 12 and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit   and the humidity sensor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode, and disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).
Disconnect these items: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Humidity/in-car temperature sensor - A/C pressure sensor
Check for continuity between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire. Go to step 16.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode, and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire. This short may have also damaged the climate control unit. Repair the short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Go to step 17.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Reconnect the climate control unit connector C (20P).

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there about 5 V? Go to step 20. Check for a loose wire or poor connection at climate control unit   connector C (20P). If the connection is good, substitute a known-good climate control unit and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

While measuring the voltage between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminal No. 2 and body ground, reconnect these items individually and note the voltage reading each time: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Humidity/in-car temperature sensor - A/C pressure sensor Does the voltage remain at about 5 V? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the component that caused the voltage drop.

dtcs.description: Climate Control Unit Lost Communication with Driver's MICU (WIPSW message)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II),   or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS.
Check the DTCs. Is DTC B2969 indicated? Go to step 6. The system is OK at this time. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the driver's MICU   and climate control unit   circuit.
Select B-CAN Control Units Information in the BODY ELECTRICAL menu.

Select Check Connected Control Units in the B-CAN Control Units Information menu. Is the driver's MICU detected? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Go to step 8.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector P (20P).
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector C (20P) and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector P (20P). Is there continuity? Go to the driver's MICU input test  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Drivers and  Passengers MICU Input Test. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the driver's MICU   and the climate control unit.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator E and Windshield Defrost: An Open in the A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2979 or E and WINDSHIELD DEFROST indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the A/C pressure sensor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the A/C pressure sensor 3P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector C (20P) and the A/C pressure sensor 3P connector. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the A/C pressure sensor.
Reconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Measure the voltage between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 1 and No. 2, and between climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 1   and No. 11. Is there about 5 V? Replace the A/C pressure sensor. Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector C (20P) and at the A/C pressure sensor 3P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator K and MODE: A Problem in the Recirculation Control Motor Circuit, Linkage, Door, or Motor

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Do the Self-diagnostic Function with the HDS   or with the climate control unit.
Check the DTCs. Is DTC B2983 or K and MODE indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the recirculation control motor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Test the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Recirculation Control Motor Test. Is the recirculation control motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Recirculation Control Motor Replacement, or repair the recirculation control linkage or door.
Disconnect the recirculation control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connector C (20P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector C (20P) and the recirculation control motor 7P connector. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor.

Check for continuity between body ground and climate control unit connector C (20P) terminals No. 9   and No. 10 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.
